TSLA: Tesla, Inc. $TSLA Shares Bought by Atlantic Union Bankshares Corp. Atlantic Union Bankshares Corp increased its stake in Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) by 4.3% in the fourth quarter, bringing its total holdings to 34,597 shares valued at $15.6 million. Despite a recent share price decline, Tesla maintains a high valuation with a P/E ratio of 397.66 and recently beat EPS estimates but missed revenue expectations. Institutional investors collectively own 66.20% of the company's stock, though analysts remain split on its future performance, with a consensus "Hold" rating and a target price of $398.42.

Ron DeSantis Shares Tesla Cybercab Spotting In Miami, Says It'll Be Interesting To See 'How Locals React'. Florida Governor Ron DeSantis shared a sighting of a Tesla Cybercab in Miami, expressing interest in its performance and local reactions. Tesla has listed Miami as a target city for robotaxi expansion in the first half of 2026, alongside other major cities. CEO Elon Musk has set ambitious production goals for the Cybercab, aiming for high-volume output and a low per-mile operating cost.

Ron DeSantis Shares Tesla Cybercab Spotting In Miami, Says It'll Be Interesting To See 'How Locals React'. Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis weighed in on a reported Tesla Cybercab sighting in Miami, expressing curiosity about its performance and local reception. Tesla is aiming for robotaxi expansion in several U.S. cities, including Miami, by the first half of 2026, with CEO Elon Musk setting ambitious production goals. The company expects volume production of the Cybercab and Semi this year, with Musk forecasting a low cost per mile, though these figures remain unproven at scale.

OKLO, NXT, TDIC, SNDK, TSLA: 5 Trending Stocks Today - SanDisk (NASDAQ:SNDK). This article highlights five trending stocks that captured investor attention. Oklo (OKLO) reported better-than-expected first-quarter results with strong cash reserves, while Nextxpower (NXT) surpassed earnings and revenue estimates and announced an acquisition. Dreamland (TDIC) surged due to a new AI-powered image library platform agreement, while SanDisk (SNDK) and Tesla (TSLA) both experienced declines, with Tesla's drop attributed to rising inflation data.

What's Going On With Tesla Stock Tuesday?. Tesla Inc. shares dropped approximately 4% on Tuesday following a broader market sell-off triggered by April's Consumer Price Index (CPI) report, which showed headline inflation climbing to 3.8% year-over-year. Geopolitical tensions, particularly an energy shock from the Strait of Hormuz blockade impacting the CPI basket, also contributed to the pressure. Despite the dip, Tesla is still trading above its key moving averages, though momentum indicators like RSI suggest it's overbought, potentially inviting profit-taking.

Inside Cathie Wood’s ARK Innovation ETF: Tesla (TSLA), Coinbase, and AMD Lead the Pack. Cathie Wood's ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) centers its investments on disruptive technologies, with top holdings including Tesla, AMD, Coinbase, and CRISPR Therapeutics. The fund's strategy focuses on AI, gene therapy, financial technology, cryptocurrency infrastructure, and digital streaming. ARKK's portfolio leans towards emerging, higher-risk growth companies, contrasting with traditional large-cap funds.

Elon Musk Sees $15 Trillion Optimus Upside — Tesla Investors Could Get A Piece For 'Free'. A Piper Sandler analyst suggests that buying Tesla stock now could give investors exposure to the Optimus humanoid robot for "free," as its potential value is not yet factored into the current stock price. Elon Musk believes Optimus could contribute significantly to Tesla's future valuation, potentially reaching a $25 trillion market cap for the company. The analyst notes that Optimus and inference-as-a-service could reshape labor markets and global GDP, making them thesis-defining products for Tesla.

TGT: Deli foods maker with Walmart, Target placements pursues $1B goal. Mama's Creations (NASDAQ: MAMA) announced its participation in four investor conferences in Spring 2026, where CEO Adam L. Michaels will engage in one-on-one meetings with institutional investors. The company highlighted ongoing integration of Crown 1, ERP consolidation, expanded production, and new national distribution deals with Walmart and Target, as it works towards its long-term objective of becoming a $1 billion deli prepared foods company.

Target (TGT) Gains As Market Dips: What You Should Know. Target (TGT) saw a 2.81% increase in its stock price, outperforming the S&P 500's daily loss despite broader market dips. The company is set to announce its earnings on May 20, 2026, with analysts predicting a rise in EPS and revenue. Target currently holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold) and shows a favorable valuation compared to its industry average.

Target pays $11.2 million for Nashville site eyed for new location. Target has paid $11.2 million for land in Nashville to build a new store, making way for the retailer to establish a presence in one of the city's largest developments. The deal was closed on May 12, 2026, and is a significant step for Target's expansion in the Greater Nashville area.

Target Corp stock (US87612E1064): UBS highlights Q1 catalyst, $5B store investment. Target Corp. is set to release its Q1 2026 earnings, which UBS analysts believe will validate the retailer's turnaround strategy. The company is also investing $5 billion in 2026 to remodel over 130 stores, open 30 new ones, and upgrade its supply chain, marking its most significant store transformation in a decade. This investment aims to boost shopper engagement and enhance its competitive position in the US retail landscape.

Key Democrat Asks Target, CVS About Consumer Data Use in Pricing. A key Democrat in Congress, Representative Frank Pallone, Jr., has initiated an inquiry into how two dozen retailers, including Target and CVS Health, use personal consumer data to set personalized prices. These companies have until May 26 to provide detailed responses regarding their use of data points such as gender, age, race, location, employment, and spending history. Pallone noted that this investigation is just the beginning and plans to extend his questions across various industries in the coming months.

Target Corp. stock (US87612E1064): UBS highlights Q1 results catalyst. UBS analysts view Target Corp.'s upcoming Q1 2026 results as a crucial catalyst to validate the retailer's turnaround progress. The stock recently traded at $121.94 with a daily gain, and while Barclays maintained an "Underweight" rating, they increased their price target to $115, suggesting evolving analyst opinions. The article emphasizes Target's strong position in the U.S. discount retail sector and its relevance for investors through its exposure to consumer spending.
SBUX: Tennessee lawmakers debate $30M incentive deal with Starbucks. Tennessee is considering a $30 million FastTrack Economic Development grant for Starbucks to establish a corporate office in Nashville, creating up to 2,000 jobs. This incentive package, which would need approval from the Tennessee State Funding Board, is part of Starbucks' "Back to Starbucks" strategy focused on expanding its U.S. presence and improving customer experience. Nashville Mayor Freddie O'Connell is also weighing a city-level economic development deal for the company.

Manhattan office with Aetna and Starbucks gets $161M refi. Vornado Realty Trust's joint venture has refinanced its 61 Ninth Avenue property in Manhattan for $161 million. The office and retail space, fully leased to Aetna and Starbucks, received an interest-only loan that extends its maturity to March 2029. This new financing replaces a previous $155 million facility and features tiered interest rates over SOFR.

Starbucks cuts jobs in Seattle as former CEO Howard Schultz blasts ‘socialist’ mayor. Starbucks is laying off 61 employees at its Seattle headquarters amidst criticism from former CEO Howard Schultz, who accused Seattle Mayor Katie Wilson of "socialist rhetoric" that targets businesses. Schultz, who recently moved to Florida, criticized Washington's tax system and reliance on sales taxes, arguing that the city's approach is driving businesses and entrepreneurs away. Seattle has also seen multiple Starbucks store closures and a decline in public safety, which Schultz believes are contributing factors to businesses leaving the city.

Tennessee set to give Starbucks $30M for 2,000-job office expansion. Starbucks Corp. is set to receive a $30 million jobs grant from the state of Tennessee for a significant expansion of its corporate office in Nashville. The company plans to hire 2,000 corporate office workers within five years, making it one of the largest job announcements in the state's history. This grant is part of a larger incentive package for the global coffee giant's move to Nashville.
DIS: Atlantic Union Bankshares Corp Reduces Holdings in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Atlantic Union Bankshares Corp reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 5.4% in the fourth quarter, selling 5,533 shares and ending with 96,125 shares valued at approximately $10.9 million. Despite this, Disney's stock appears to be gaining momentum due to strong earnings, beating quarterly EPS and revenue estimates, and upbeat analyst sentiment, with several firms raising price targets and one upgrading the stock to "strong-buy". Institutional investors and hedge funds collectively own 65.71% of Disney stock, with some increasing their positions while Atlantic Union Bankshares Corp decreased its holdings.

Fandom Takes Center Stage as Disney’s Sports, TV and Film Powerhouses Command the North Javits Center. The Walt Disney Company held its 2026 Upfront presentation, featuring CEO Josh D’Amaro's debut and a lineup of over 100 stars from Disney's franchises. The event showcased the company's extensive entertainment and sports portfolio, highlighting upcoming content, major cultural moments in 2027 like the Super Bowl LXI and the Oscars, and new series announcements for Disney+ and Hulu. The presentation concluded with a performance by GRAMMY®-winner Olivia Rodrigo, emphasizing Disney's commitment to fandom, storytelling, and innovation.

'The Punisher: One Last Kill': Behind the Scenes of Marvel Television's Dark, Action-Packed Special Presentation on Disney+. This article goes behind the scenes of Marvel Television's new special presentation, "The Punisher: One Last Kill," premiering on Disney+. Director Reinaldo Marcus Green discusses collaborating with star Jon Bernthal, the character's dark journey, and integrating real veteran experiences into the story. The special aims to offer a gritty, character-driven action experience distinct from other MCU street-level narratives.

Netflix vs Disney: Only One Is a Winner of The Streaming War. This article compares the Q1 FY26 performance of Netflix and the fiscal Q2 FY26 performance of Disney, highlighting their differing strategies in the streaming war. Netflix is focusing on a leaner, software-style operation with a successful ad-supported tier, while Disney is broadening its portfolio through parks, IP-driven streaming, and strategic acquisitions. The author suggests leaning towards Disney due to its streaming profitability milestone and record parks revenue, despite Netflix's cleaner business model.
NKE: Saucony appoints former Nike executive Wendy Kula as CMO. Saucony has appointed Wendy Kula, formerly Nike's vice president of women’s brand marketing for North America, as its new Chief Marketing Officer. This strategic hire aims to enhance Saucony's presence in performance running and lifestyle categories. The company anticipates Kula's expertise in culture, community, and storytelling will be key to its global brand growth and consumer engagement efforts.

Nike Tariff Lawsuit Puts Pricing Practices And Investor Risks In Focus. A class action lawsuit has been filed against Nike (NYSE:NKE) alleging the company raised prices on footwear and apparel to offset import tariffs and did not issue refunds when the tariffs were removed. This case places Nike's pricing transparency under scrutiny and could significantly impact its brand trust, financial performance, and future pricing strategies. Investors are advised to monitor the lawsuit's progress and Nike's response, as it could have implications for cash flow, legal expenses, and potentially influence how other consumer brands handle tariff-related costs.
